content.thinking:
I found a definite defect in AgentCta.tsx line 23: `

 

` - This is effectively an empty paragraph (just a non-breaking space). There's supposed to be descriptive text under the CTA heading "Start shipping with agents." but the body text paragraph is just a non-breaking space. Now let me check the pricing and terminal pages.

1 "use client"; 2 3 import { useState } from "react"; 4 import Link from "next/link"; 5 import PlusIcon from "@/components/icons/PlusIcon"; 6 import MinusIcon from "@/components/icons/MinusIcon"; 7 8 interface FAQItem { 9 question: string; 10 answer: React.ReactNode; 11 } 12 13 const faqs: FAQItem[] = [ 14 { 15 question: "What counts as a credit in Warp?", 16 answer: ( 17 <> 18

19 Interactions with agents in Warp consume credits through a combination of inference costs 20 and Platform fees. Inference costs are charged for any use of the Warp Agent and are based 21 on the tokens used to complete the task. Platform fees cover the cost of compute when 22 running cloud agents or routing requests through the Warp server. 23

24

25 Credit usage depends on factors like the model used, the size of the task, the amount of 26 context gathered, codebase size, and whether the agent runs locally or in the cloud. 27

28 29 ), 30 }, 31 { 32 question: "Does Warp have a SOC 2 Type 2 attestation?", 33 answer: ( 34

35 Yes, Warp has obtained a SOC 2 Type 2 attestation from an accredited third party. Visit{" "} 36 37 Warp's Trust Center 38 {" "} 39 to request the report. 40

41 ), 42 }, 43 { 44 question: "Should I subscribe to the Build plan, Max plan, or the Business plan?", 45 answer: ( 46 <> 47

48 Build is a good fit for individual developers or small teams that want flexible access to 49 Warp AI. It includes 1,500 monthly credits, Reload credits, full Warp Agent access, BYOK, 50 custom inference endpoint support, collaboration features, and higher codebase indexing 51 limits. 52

53

54 Max is best for heavy AI users who consistently need more capacity. It includes 18,000 55 monthly credits, 10× the included credits of Build, and a 17% lower effective credit rate 56 than the best Reload credit tier. 57

58

59 Business is best for teams that need SSO, team usage metrics, admin-configurable data 60 controls, and centralized spend management. Business supports up to 25 team members and 61 includes 1,500 credits per seat per month. 62

63

64 For larger organizations that need unlimited seats, advanced governance, custom credits and 65 usage terms, Enterprise Analytics API, BYOLLM, per-user spend controls, or self-hosted 66 cloud agents,{" "} 67 68 contact us 69 {" "} 70 about Enterprise. 71

72 73 ), 74 }, 75 { 76 question: "How can I subscribe to a Warp Business or Enterprise plan?", 77 answer: ( 78 <> 79

80 You can subscribe to the Business plan directly from our{" "} 81 82 upgrade page 83 {" "} 84 or via your in-app Settings > Billing & usage page. 85

86

87 For larger engineering organizations or companies that need advanced compliance, 88 governance, custom usage terms, or dedicated support, Warp also offers an Enterprise plan 89 with custom pricing and deployment options. To learn more, contact us at{" "} 90 91 sales@warp.dev 92 {" "} 93 or{" "} 94 95 submit a request 96 97 . 98

99 100 ), 101 }, 102 { 103 question: "Does Warp have Zero Data Retention policies with LLM providers?", 104 answer: ( 105 <> 106

107 Warp integrates with multiple Large Language Model (LLM) providers to power its 108 AI-driven features. 109

110

These providers include, but are not limited to:

111
    112
  • Anthropic
    • 113
  • OpenAI
    • 114
  • Google
    • 115
  • Fireworks AI
    • 116
117

118 Warp has executed Zero Data Retention (ZDR) agreements with these providers. This means 119 that, by default across all plans: 120

121
    122
  • LLM providers commit not to train their models on any customer-generated data processed through Warp's services.
    • 123
  • LLM providers commit to delete inputs and outputs after generating the relevant output, within a fixed time period.
    • 124
125

Warp enforces these commitments through both technical measures and contractual safeguards with the LLM providers.

126 127 ), 128 }, 129 { 130 question: "How can I enable Zero Data Retention in Warp?", 131 answer: ( 132 <> 133

Zero Data Retention (ZDR) can be enabled in two ways:

134
    135
  • Individual level — Any user can enable full ZDR for their own account by disabling Help Improve Warp in their Warp settings under Privacy.
    • 136
  • Organization-wide — On the Business and Enterprise plans, admins can enforce ZDR for all members by default via the admin panel, ensuring team-wide compliance without relying on individual settings.
    • 137
138

139 If you'd like to request organization-wide ZDR, please contact your Warp sales 140 representative at{" "} 141 142 sales@warp.dev 143 {" "} 144 or submit a request via our{" "} 145 146 Contact Sales form 147 148 . 149

150

151 Regardless of plan, Warp never allows OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or other model 152 providers to store, retain, or train their models on your data. Warp has full Zero Data 153 Retention policies with all LLM providers. 154

155 156 ), 157 }, 158 { 159 question: "Do paid plans support additional AI usage beyond the included credits?", 160 answer: ( 161 <> 162

Yes. Build, Max, and Business support additional AI usage through Reload credits.

163

164 After a user's included monthly credits are used, they can continue using AI features 165 with Reload credits. Reload credits are tied to individual users, roll over month to month, 166 remain valid for 12 months, and offer discounts for larger denominations. 167

168

Team admins can configure team-wide spend caps and auto-reload settings for Reload credit usage.

169 170 ), 171 }, 172 { 173 question: "How often do AI credit limits reset?", 174 answer: ( 175 <> 176

177 Your included plan credits reset every 30 days from your subscription or renewal date. 178 You can view your remaining balance under Settings > Billing and usage. 179

180

181 If you run out of credits before your next reset, you can purchase Reload credits to keep 182 using AI features without interruption. On eligible plans, admins can also configure 183 auto-reload and team-wide spend caps. 184

185 186 ), 187 }, 188 { 189 question: "How does auto-reload work for teams?", 190 answer: ( 191 <> 192

On Build, Max, and Business, team admins can configure auto-reload for Reload credit usage.

193

194 When auto-reload is ON, the admin selects the Reload credit denomination for the team. 195 Warp automatically reloads credits when a user's credit pool drops below the 196 threshold, subject to the team-wide spend cap. 197

198

199 When auto-reload is OFF, eligible team members can purchase Reload credits manually, as 200 long as the team remains below the team-wide spend cap. 201

202 203 ), 204 }, 205 { 206 question: "How do Reload credits work for multi-seat teams?", 207 answer: ( 208 <> 209

210 For teams on Build, Max, or Business, included monthly credits are tied to each paid seat 211 and reset monthly based on the subscription or renewal date. 212

213

214 Reload credits are also tied to individual users rather than pooled across the team. If a 215 user runs out of included credits, they can purchase additional credits that will be tied 216 to their account and purchased via the billing method on file. 217

218

Team admins can configure team-wide spend caps and auto-reload settings to manage overall Reload credit usage.

219

220 Existing pooled Reload credits purchased before this change remain available and will be 221 used first. After those credits are used, new Reload credits will be attributed to 222 individual users. 223

224 225 ), 226 }, 227 ]; 228 229 230 export default function PricingFAQ() { 231 const [openIndex, setOpenIndex] = useState(null); 232 233 return ( 234

268 ); 269 } 270

"use client"; import { useState } from "react"; import Link from "next/link"; import PlusIcon from "@/components/icons/PlusIcon"; import MinusIcon from "@/components/icons/MinusIcon"; interface FAQItem { question: string; answer: React.ReactNode; } const faqs: FAQItem[] = [ { question: "What counts as a credit in Warp?", answer: ( <>

Interactions with agents in Warp consume credits through a combination of inference costs and Platform fees. Inference costs are charged for any use of the Warp Agent and are based on the tokens used to complete the task. Platform fees cover the cost of compute when running cloud agents or routing requests through the Warp server.

Credit usage depends on factors like the model used, the size of the task, the amount of context gathered, codebase size, and whether the agent runs locally or in the cloud.

), }, { question: "Does Warp have a SOC 2 Type 2 attestation?", answer: (

Yes, Warp has obtained a SOC 2 Type 2 attestation from an accredited third party. Visit{" "} Warp's Trust Center {" "} to request the report.

), }, { question: "Should I subscribe to the Build plan, Max plan, or the Business plan?", answer: ( <>

Build is a good fit for individual developers or small teams that want flexible access to Warp AI. It includes 1,500 monthly credits, Reload credits, full Warp Agent access, BYOK, custom inference endpoint support, collaboration features, and higher codebase indexing limits.

Max is best for heavy AI users who consistently need more capacity. It includes 18,000 monthly credits, 10× the included credits of Build, and a 17% lower effective credit rate than the best Reload credit tier.

Business is best for teams that need SSO, team usage metrics, admin-configurable data controls, and centralized spend management. Business supports up to 25 team members and includes 1,500 credits per seat per month.

For larger organizations that need unlimited seats, advanced governance, custom credits and usage terms, Enterprise Analytics API, BYOLLM, per-user spend controls, or self-hosted cloud agents,{" "} contact us {" "} about Enterprise.

), }, { question: "How can I subscribe to a Warp Business or Enterprise plan?", answer: ( <>

You can subscribe to the Business plan directly from our{" "} upgrade page {" "} or via your in-app Settings > Billing & usage page.

For larger engineering organizations or companies that need advanced compliance, governance, custom usage terms, or dedicated support, Warp also offers an Enterprise plan with custom pricing and deployment options. To learn more, contact us at{" "} sales@warp.dev {" "} or{" "} submit a request .

), }, { question: "Does Warp have Zero Data Retention policies with LLM providers?", answer: ( <>

Warp integrates with multiple Large Language Model (LLM) providers to power its AI-driven features.

These providers include, but are not limited to:

  • Anthropic
  • OpenAI
  • Google
  • Fireworks AI

Warp has executed Zero Data Retention (ZDR) agreements with these providers. This means that, by default across all plans:

  • LLM providers commit not to train their models on any customer-generated data processed through Warp's services.
  • LLM providers commit to delete inputs and outputs after generating the relevant output, within a fixed time period.

Warp enforces these commitments through both technical measures and contractual safeguards with the LLM providers.

), }, { question: "How can I enable Zero Data Retention in Warp?", answer: ( <>

Zero Data Retention (ZDR) can be enabled in two ways:

  • Individual level — Any user can enable full ZDR for their own account by disabling Help Improve Warp in their Warp settings under Privacy.
  • Organization-wide — On the Business and Enterprise plans, admins can enforce ZDR for all members by default via the admin panel, ensuring team-wide compliance without relying on individual settings.

If you'd like to request organization-wide ZDR, please contact your Warp sales representative at{" "} sales@warp.dev {" "} or submit a request via our{" "} Contact Sales form .

Regardless of plan, Warp never allows OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or other model providers to store, retain, or train their models on your data. Warp has full Zero Data Retention policies with all LLM providers.

), }, { question: "Do paid plans support additional AI usage beyond the included credits?", answer: ( <>

Yes. Build, Max, and Business support additional AI usage through Reload credits.

After a user's included monthly credits are used, they can continue using AI features with Reload credits. Reload credits are tied to individual users, roll over month to month, remain valid for 12 months, and offer discounts for larger denominations.

Team admins can configure team-wide spend caps and auto-reload settings for Reload credit usage.

), }, { question: "How often do AI credit limits reset?", answer: ( <>

Your included plan credits reset every 30 days from your subscription or renewal date. You can view your remaining balance under Settings > Billing and usage.

If you run out of credits before your next reset, you can purchase Reload credits to keep using AI features without interruption. On eligible plans, admins can also configure auto-reload and team-wide spend caps.

), }, { question: "How does auto-reload work for teams?", answer: ( <>

On Build, Max, and Business, team admins can configure auto-reload for Reload credit usage.

When auto-reload is ON, the admin selects the Reload credit denomination for the team. Warp automatically reloads credits when a user's credit pool drops below the threshold, subject to the team-wide spend cap.

When auto-reload is OFF, eligible team members can purchase Reload credits manually, as long as the team remains below the team-wide spend cap.

), }, { question: "How do Reload credits work for multi-seat teams?", answer: ( <>

For teams on Build, Max, or Business, included monthly credits are tied to each paid seat and reset monthly based on the subscription or renewal date.

Reload credits are also tied to individual users rather than pooled across the team. If a user runs out of included credits, they can purchase additional credits that will be tied to their account and purchased via the billing method on file.

Team admins can configure team-wide spend caps and auto-reload settings to manage overall Reload credit usage.

Existing pooled Reload credits purchased before this change remain available and will be used first. After those credits are used, new Reload credits will be attributed to individual users.

), }, ]; export default function PricingFAQ() { const [openIndex, setOpenIndex] = useState(null); return (

Pricing FAQ

{faqs.map((faq, i) => { const isOpen = openIndex === i; return (
{faq.answer}
); })}
); }

content.thinking:
I noticed something in `PricingFAQ.tsx`:

Looking at the Max plan description in the FAQ:
> "Max is best for heavy AI users who consistently need more capacity. It includes 18,000 monthly credits, 10× the included credits of Build, and a 17% lower effective credit rate than the best Reload credit tier."

But in `PricingCards.tsx`, the Max plan feature list says:
> "12× the included credits of Build"

So the FAQ says "10× the included credits of Build" while the pricing card says "12× the included credits of Build". This is a copy defect — brand-mismatched/inconsistent numbers.


Let me verify the math here. If Build has 1,500 credits and Max has 18,000, that's 18,000 ÷ 1,500 = 12×, so the cards are correct and the FAQ has the wrong multiplier. I'm spotting another issue in AgentCta.tsx where there's just a non-breaking space placeholder in the paragraph — the descriptive copy that should be there is completely missing, leaving an empty structure behind.

content.text: I found a potential number discrepancy in PricingFAQ. Let me check the remaining terminal components.

content.thinking:
Now let me confirm the discrepancy I found in the FAQ. In `PricingFAQ.tsx` (line 54-56):
```
Max is best for heavy AI users who consistently need more capacity. It includes 18,000
monthly credits, 10× the included credits of Build, and a 17% lower effective credit rate
```

But in `PricingCards.tsx` (line 130-131):
```
{
  text: "12× the included credits of Build",
```

And Build includes 1,500 credits per month (line 99 in PricingCards). 18,000 / 1,500 = 12, so the PricingCards.tsx claim of 12× is correct, but the FAQ says "10×". That's a copy defect — inconsistent numbers.


I'm also noticing that AgentCta.tsx has an empty paragraph tag where there should be body copy under the main CTA heading. Let me quickly scan through the Banner and Partners components to see if there are any other issues there.

content.thinking:
Now I have enough information to render my verdict. Let me summarize the findings: 1. **AgentCta.tsx:23** — `

 

`: This is an empty/blank paragraph serving as placeholder. There's a descriptive text section under the "Start shipping with agents." heading but the paragraph contains only a non-breaking space (` `). This is effectively empty copy where a real description was expected. This qualifies as "Empty or truncated strings." 2. **PricingFAQ.tsx:55** — The FAQ says "10× the included credits of Build" but the pricing card (PricingCards.tsx:131) says "12× the included credits of Build". Looking at the actual numbers, Build has 1,500 credits and Max has 18,000 credits, which works out to exactly 12 times more. The FAQ's claim of "10×" is incorrect—it should be "12×" to match the pricing card and the actual math. The math confirms the pricing card is correct—the FAQ contains a factual error with "10×" instead of "12×". This is a clear discrepancy that should be flagged as a defect, likely under the inconsistency category since it's a high-visibility pricing comparison.

content.text:
I have enough data to render my verdict. Two defects found: 1. `AgentCta.tsx:23` — the descriptive paragraph under the CTA heading is `

 

`, a bare non-breaking space standing in for real body copy. 2. `PricingFAQ.tsx:55` — the Max plan description says "10× the included credits of Build" but the pricing card (PricingCards.tsx:131) says "12× the included credits of Build". Build has 1,500 credits; 18,000 / 1,500 = 12, so the card is correct and the FAQ has a wrong multiplier. {"verdict":"fail","findings":[{"message":"Empty/placeholder body copy","evidence":"AgentCta.tsx:23 — `
